customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety on this call — Tiffany was cooperative and booked without resistance. Primary practical complication is billing: she recently moved in, the previous occupants had bypassed the meter, and Pepco issued a ~$1,000 guesstimate bill that does not reflect actual usage. She is waiting for a corrected bill. No emotional distress surfaced; she disclosed the billing situation matter-of-factly.
unresolved_concerns: No actual utility bill available — Pepco guesstimate of ~$1,000 is not a real usage figure; corrected bill has not yet been received
System sizing was based on square footage (5,500 sq ft) only — no kWh data, no pool/EV load confirmation
Pool or EV ownership was raised as a hypothetical but never answered — load profile is incomplete
sensitive_topics_raised: Recent move-in — previous occupants had bypassed the electric meter; Pepco had to re-hook the connection
Guesstimate bill of ~$1,000 — customer knows it's not real and is waiting for correction
exact_promises_rep_made: Specialist will meet with Tiffany on Wednesday the 22nd at 9AM
Specialist will show an evaluation for the home and explain how the program works
State and local incentives will be covered in the appointment
closer_should_know: There is no utility bill on file and no kWh data — the design will need to be built from square footage (5,500 sq ft) and whatever load information the closer can gather at the start of the appointment. Confirm pool/EV ownership early; it was raised but never answered on the setter call. The $1,000 Pepco figure is a guesstimate anomaly, not a real bill — do not anchor on it without first establishing actual usage.
